implementation_md_format.overview: This file defines the sample structure (not mandatory to follow) and content requirements for implementation.md. Generate this file ONLY after the satisfaction gate in phase 8 of the questioning protocol is passed. This document is the single source of truth for multi-agent-system-builder mode. It must be complete enough that no additional questions are needed during generation.

section.name: system_overview
section.heading: ## System Overview

required_content.item: One paragraph: what the system does and who uses it
Primary use cases (3–5 bullet points)
What the system explicitly does NOT do (out of scope)
High-level architecture diagram in Mermaid (agent topology)

section.mermaid_diagram_note: Use graph TD or flowchart. Show agents as nodes, communication as edges. Label edges with data type or protocol (e.g., "JSON: UserRequest"). Avoid double quotes inside brackets — use single quotes in Mermaid labels.

section.name: agent_inventory
section.heading: ## Agent Inventory
section.required_content: One entry per agent in the following format:
section.agent_entry_format:
### `agent_name`
| Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Role | What this agent is responsible for | | Model | model-name (e.g., llama3.2 — bare Ollama name, no prefix) | | Tools | tool_one, tool_two (comma-separated) | | Receives | Input type and format | | Returns | Output type and format | | Style | react / custom — react = create_react_agent (tool-using, autonomous); custom = async node function (single LLM call, deterministic). See 3_agent_design.xml two_node_types. |
**Responsibilities:** - Specific responsibility 1 - Specific responsibility 2
**NOT responsible for:** - Explicit exclusion 1 - Explicit exclusion 2
**System Prompt Summary:** Brief description of the agent's persona, constraints, and output format expectations.

section.name: orchestration_topology
section.heading: ## Orchestration Topology

required_content.item: Pattern: supervisor-worker / pipeline / peer-to-peer / swarm
Entry point: which agent receives the first request
Routing logic: how requests are directed to the correct agent
Communication: how agents pass results to each other
Sequence diagram in Mermaid for the primary workflow

section.sequence_diagram_note: Use sequenceDiagram. Show actor/user, all agents involved, and external systems. Show both the happy path and at least one error path.

section.name: data_flow
section.heading: ## Data Flow and Contracts

required_content.item: Pydantic model definitions (or equivalent schema) for all data passed between agents
Session/context structure: what is stored, how it is passed
External data sources: what APIs or databases agents call
Data validation responsibility: which component validates at each boundary

section.name: api_design
section.heading: ## API Design
section.endpoint_format:
### `POST /api/v1/chat`
| Field | Value | |-------|-------| | Purpose | Send a message to the multi-agent system | | Auth | Bearer token / API key / none | | Request | `ChatRequest` Pydantic model | | Response | `ChatResponse` Pydantic model | | Errors | 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 503 Agent Unavailable | | Streaming | Yes / No |

also_include.item: WebSocket endpoints if streaming is required
Authentication mechanism and where credentials come from

section.name: provider_configuration
section.heading: ## Provider Configuration

required_content.item: Ollama base URL (default: http://localhost:11434)
Model assignments: which agent uses which model
Ollama generation parameters per agent (temperature, num_predict, etc.)
Environment variables table

section.env_table_format: | Variable | Required | Default | Description | |----------|----------|---------|-------------| | OLLAMA_BASE_URL | No | http://localhost:11434 | Ollama server URL | | DEFAULT_MODEL | No | llama3.2 | Default Ollama model (bare name, no prefix) | | LLM_TEMPERATURE | No | 0.7 | Default LLM temperature | | OLLAMA_NUM_CTX | No | 4096 | Context window tokens (Ollama default 2048 is too small) | | OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE | No | 5m | How long to keep model loaded between calls | | API_KEY | No | (empty) | API key for auth — leave empty to disable | | LOG_LEVEL | No | INFO | Logging verbosity |
section.model_assignment_note: Document which agent uses which model. Example: supervisor uses phi4:mini (fast, low RAM); research agent uses llama3.2 (better reasoning). Add one config field per agent: SUPERVISOR_MODEL=phi4:mini, RESEARCH_MODEL=llama3.2, etc. Bare model names only — no "ollama/" prefix.

section.name: project_structure
section.heading: ## Project Structure
section.required_content: Full directory tree of files to be generated, with a one-line comment on each file.
section.format: ``` project_root/ ├── .bob/ # Bob configuration (already exists) ├── backend/ │ ├── app/ │ │ ├── main.py # FastAPI app entry point, graph compiled in lifespan │ │ ├── config.py # Pydantic settings, env var loading │ │ ├── llm.py # get_llm() factory — ChatOllama instance per call │ │ ├── api/ │ │ │ └── v1/ │ │ │ └── routes.py # API routes — call graph.ainvoke() with thread_id │ │ ├── graph/ │ │ │ ├── state.py # State TypedDict classes (SupervisorState, etc.) │ │ │ ├── nodes.py # Graph nodes — one per agent from Agent Inventory │ │ │ └── builder.py # StateGraph construction + compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver()) │ │ ├── tools/ │ │ │ └── domain_tools.py # @tool decorated functions (one file per tool domain) │ │ └── models/ │ │ └── schemas.py # API Pydantic models (ChatRequest, ChatResponse, etc.) │ ├── tests/ │ │ ├── conftest.py # Shared fixtures (mock_llm) │ │ └── test_graph.py # Node unit tests using patched LLM │ ├── pyproject.toml # pytest config — asyncio_mode = "auto" │ └── requirements.txt ├── frontend/ # React + Vite (generated separately) ├── backend/ │ ├── Dockerfile # Python 3.11-slim container — builds backend │ ├── .dockerignore # Excludes .env, __pycache__, .venv │ ... ├── frontend/ │ ├── Dockerfile # Node 20-slim container — builds frontend │ ├── .dockerignore # Excludes node_modules, .env │ ... ├── docker-compose.yml # Orchestrates backend + frontend — single docker compose up ├── docker-compose.yml # Start: docker compose up --build └── implementation.md # This file ```

required_content.item: Retry policy: max retries, backoff strategy for each failure type
Timeout policy: per-agent and per-workflow timeouts
Fallback behaviour: what happens when an agent fails
Ollama failure handling: what the system does if Ollama is unreachable
Error response format: what the API returns on failure

section.name: open_decisions
section.heading: ## Open Decisions
section.purpose: List any design decisions that were deliberately deferred or left configurable. This section may be empty if everything was fully decided.
section.format: | Decision | Options Considered | Chosen Approach | Reason | |----------|-------------------|-----------------|--------| | Session persistence | In-memory vs Redis | In-memory for MVP | Simplicity |

generation_rules.rule: Do NOT generate implementation.md until the satisfaction gate in phase 8 of 1_questioning_protocol.xml has been explicitly passed by the user.

Every required section must be present and populated. No placeholder text such as "TBD", "to be determined", or "will figure out later". If something is truly configurable, document it as a config value with a default.

The required_sections above are the MINIMUM structured summary — they ensure multi-agent-system-builder mode has machine-readable anchors to generate from. They are NOT the ceiling. Write as much detail as you have clarity on. After the required sections, continue writing freely: anything that captures the full picture of what was discussed. A richer implementation.md means less guesswork during generation.
Mermaid diagrams must be valid. Do not use double quotes inside square brackets. Use single quotes for labels. Test syntax mentally before writing.
Pydantic models in the Data Flow section must match the actual models that will be generated in the backend. This document is the contract.
After writing implementation.md, tell the user: "implementation.md is ready." Do not switch modes automatically.

implementation_md_format.extended_content_guidance:
These are sections you SHOULD add after the required sections if the architecture warrants them. They are not mandatory but make the implementation.md significantly more useful. Write them in whatever format best captures the detail.
What was considered and why it was accepted or rejected. Example: "We considered event sourcing for the meeting data pipeline but chose batch processing because real-time latency is not a requirement for this system." This prevents future developers from re-litigating closed decisions.
For each agent, the reasoning behind its system prompt: what constraints were imposed, what output format was chosen and why, what edge cases the prompt guards against. The actual prompt text if it is stable enough to write now.
Beyond the Pydantic models: how data flows through the full pipeline end-to-end, what transformations happen at each stage, where data is persisted vs. passed in-memory, and what happens to data that fails validation at each boundary.
If a dashboard or UI is part of the system: component breakdown, what data each component needs from the API, real-time vs. polling requirements, key interactions the user must be able to perform, and any specific visual behaviour (charts, maps, live updates) that affects the backend contract.
If the full system is too large for one sprint: what is the MVP that proves the core value, what comes in phase 2, and what is deliberately out of scope for now. Helps the builder know what to implement first and what to stub.
Beyond the Open Decisions table: deeper risks, assumptions that have not been validated, external dependencies that could block progress, and known unknowns.
